title: NOC 72010 – Contractors and supervisors, machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting trades and related occupations | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors (NOC 72010) are the tactical leaders of Canada's industrial manufacturing and construction sectors. As the country focuses on "Technological Sovereignty" and massive infrastructure upgrades, these professionals are responsible for the safe and efficient operation of machine shops and metal fabrication sites. Unlike general managers, these supervisors are deeply technical—often "Journeypersons" themselves—overseeing the precision work of machinists, sheet metal workers, and tool and die makers. In 2026, the role increasingly involves managing AI-integrated CNC workflows and ensuring compliance with the latest "Net-Zero" industrial safety standards.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires a college diploma, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Operational Coordination: Supervising, coordinating, and scheduling the activities of workers who shape, join, and machine metal parts or structures.
Technical Guidance: Setting up machines and equipment and writing or troubleshooting programs for Computer Numerically Controlled (CNC) machine tools.
Resource Management: Requisitioning materials and supplies and resolving work problems to improve productivity and quality.
Personnel Actions: Training workers, recommending hirings and promotions, and ensuring all standards for safe working conditions are strictly observed.
Contract Management: (For Contractors) Managing the operations of their own companies, including bidding on projects and overseeing client relations.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Machinist or Tool and Die Maker are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in machining, tool and die making, or another related metalworking trade is usually required.
Skills: Proficiency in CADD software, reading engineering drawings, and advanced blueprint interpretation.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is characterized by a "Moderate to Steady" demand, with a high volume of openings driven by the retirement of an aging skilled workforce:

Ontario: Limited to Moderate. In March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72010, specifically focusing on the GTA and Southwestern Ontario regions to support the automotive and aerospace clusters.
Alberta: Strong Demand. Home to Canada's energy and industrial manufacturing sectors, Alberta offers the highest median wages for this NOC, averaging $44.44/hr ($93,790/year).

British Columbia: Moderate. Demand is steady in the "Mainland/Southwest" region, particularly in specialized marine and aerospace fabrication. BC anticipates over 2,900 openings in this sector through 2035.

National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. While overall job growth is modest, the replacement of retirees is the primary driver for international recruitment through 2033.
Why Work in Canada as a Metal Trades Supervisor?
Technical Leadership: Direct high-precision projects using 2026-grade technologies, including multi-axis CNC machining and automated metal-forming systems.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is significantly higher than general laborers, with top-end wages in Alberta reaching $75.00/hr.
Entrepreneurial Opportunity: A significant portion of this NOC includes Contractors who own and operate their own machine shops or fabrication businesses.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a core target for Federal Skilled Trades and various Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On March 25, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in this NOC under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with a valid job offer in Ontario's industrial sectors.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 technical trade, NOC 72010 is eligible for specialized "Trades" draws, which often have lower CRS requirements than general draws.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed initiative for 2026–2027 targeting up to 33,000 temporary workers. Supervisors in "Priority Sectors" like manufacturing and construction are top-tier candidates.
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for those with a high-wage offer in British Columbia’s fabrication or aerospace manufacturing industries.
